task_completion.description: Log in to the FreeMED application using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the FreeMED login screen with 'admin' entered, followed by the FreeMED dashboard indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the reference dictionaries or specialties section in FreeMED.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeMED interface should display a menu or page specifically for 'Reference Dictionaries', 'Specialties', or 'Provider Specialties'.
The text 'Gastroenterology' should be visible inside the 'Specialty' or 'Name' input field on the add/edit specialty form.
The text '207RG0100X' should be visible inside the 'Code' or 'Taxonomy' input field on the form.
The text 'Digestive System Specialist' should be visible inside the 'Description' input field on the form.

task_completion.description: Save the new specialty to the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A click on a 'Save' or 'Add' button, followed by a success message, or the new 'Gastroenterology' specialty appearing as an entry in the reference dictionary list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeMED web interface rather than using terminal commands or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show a web browser navigating the FreeMED UI. There should be no terminal windows executing curl, SQL, or python scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual FreeMED application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar should show the correct local address for the FreeMED application, not a local HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a natural progression of navigating, typing, and clicking, without the browser's 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools pane open to manipulate the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must explicitly create a specialty with the Name 'Gastroenterology', the Taxonomy Code '207RG0100X', and the Description 'Digestive System Specialist'.
